name: node-es-abstract
title: node-es-abstract
description:
ECMAScript spec abstract operations
When different versions of the spec conflict, the default export will be the
latest version of the abstract operation.
.
All abstract operations will also be available under an es5/es2015/es2016
entry point, and exported property, if you require a specific version.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-es-abstract because ECMAScript spec abstract operations.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-es-abstract as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-es-abstract with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-es-abstract --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
